q1.intitule: What are key motivations behind smart mobility development?
propositions: Reducing the need for public transport
q1.reponse: Increasing efficiency, safety and quality of transport
q1.solution: Why is ITS implemented? Increasing the efficiency… Increasing security… Improving quality…

q2.intitule: Which elements are part of an Intelligent Transport System (ITS)?
q2.reponse: Fleet, Infrastructure, Human Resources, Law regulations
q2.solution: Intelligent transport system… Fleet, Infrastructure, HR, Law regulations.

q3.intitule: Which of the following is a V2I functionality?
q3.reponse: Collecting and comparing data from vehicles to map and manage incidents
q3.solution: V2I: Collect and compare data from vehicles to help map and manage incidents.

q4.intitule: Which of the following belongs to V2V communication?

q5.intitule: Which IPTS system provides real-time vehicle location?
q5.solution: AVLS – provide real-time information about PuT vehicles – location, speed…

q6.intitule: Which standard is used for real-time public transport data?
q6.solution: SIRI – real time data and GTFS-RT.

q7.intitule: Which standard defines structured static transport information (timetables)?

q8.intitule: According to SAE J3016, differences between levels 1–5 relate mainly to:
q8.reponse: How responsibility for safe movement is shared between ADAS and driver
q8.solution: Above Level 1… differences are related to how responsibility… is divided/shared between ADAS and driver.

q9.intitule: What elements are part of a transport system?
q9.reponse: Infrastructure, rolling stock, human resources, information flows, principles of organization
q9.solution: Transport System – a set of elements involved in the process of moving people and/or goods… infrastructure, rolling stock, human resources, information flows, principles of organization.

q10.intitule: Which activities are included in transport management?
q10.reponse: Planning, organizing, giving orders, coordination, controlling
q10.solution: Management – activities aimed at the effective use of available resources… planning and decision-making, organizing, giving orders, coordination, controlling.

q11.intitule: Which of the following is a main question addressed by transport management?
q11.reponse: How to create safe and reliable transport systems for passengers and cargo
q11.solution: Transport Management seeks to answer questions like: How to create transport systems and infrastructure that are safe and reliable…

q12.intitule: Which of the following is an example of a transport decision-making problem?
propositions: Choosing the most popular vehicle brand
q12.reponse: Minimum cost of transporting goods from m origins to n destinations
q12.solution: Transport problem – finding the minimum cost of transportation of goods from m origin to n destination.

q13.intitule: Which of these is also considered in transport management decision problems?
q13.reponse: Fleet sizing and composition, vehicle scheduling, location of hubs, scheduling drivers, ranking service providers
q13.solution: Fleet sizing and fleet composition, vehicle scheduling, location of objects… scheduling drivers’ work, selecting best logistics service provider.

q14.intitule: Which function of management is applied to the transport problem (minimum cost)?
q14.solution: Decision Problems – Planning & Organizing: Transport problem + +

q15.intitule: Which level of transport modeling focuses on individual vehicles and interactions?
q15.solution: Micro: high accuracy – particular entities, interactions between vehicles, pedestrians, infrastructure. e.g., PTV Vissim, Aimsun.

q16.intitule: Macro transport models are mainly used for:
q16.reponse: High aggregation traffic flows over large transportation systems
q16.solution: Macro: high aggregation – traffic flows, large transportation systems. e.g., PTV Visum, EMME.

q17.intitule: Which method is considered primary data collection in transport surveys?
q17.solution: Primary Data Collection: field surveys, interviews, measuring, counting.

q18.intitule: What is the purpose of a CTS?
q18.reponse: Inventory of movement of people and vehicles, and examination of transport behaviors
q18.solution: CTS – ‘inventory’ of the movement of people and vehicles, examination of transport behaviors in a separate territorial unit.

q19.intitule: Which of the following is typically included in a CTS?
q19.reponse: Travel behavior survey, traffic volume counts, speed data, vehicle classification, origin-destination surveys, travel time studies, parking studies
q19.solution: CTS typically includes: Travel Behavior Survey, Traffic Volume Counts, Speed Data, Vehicle Classification, Origin-Destination Surveys, Travel Time and Delay Studies, Parking Studies, etc.

q20.intitule: Which rule is important when delimiting a CTS research area?
q20.reponse: Most journeys related to the study area should begin and end in the area
q20.solution: The majority of journeys related to the future study area should have their beginning and end in the research area.
